name: Fungus Strung Bow
description: Arrows loosed from it always seem to grow something where they land.

name: Lacquered Bow
description: The Archers of Halheim say a bow must be held as if it were the nape of an animal's neck. Tender, and gentle - but ready to send a message at any moment.

name: Woodland Protector
description: Made from the antlers of a whole herd of deer, it brings luck only to those who hunt for food, not sport.

name: Patience
description: The woodland bow of a hunter so patient, moss eventually overgrew him, leaving only the bow behind.

name: Twined Recurve Bow
description: A bow of unconventional design that musters up considerable strength from its short draw.

name: Answered Prayer
description: It's said a betrayed archer spent years hunting down her former allies and impaling their skulls on her bow.

name: Pale Ash
description: A bow of eerie white that never seems to dirty - mud and blood alike slide off it like water.

name: Balak Talon
description: Lined with a hush of rare feathers, this bow seems to vanish when leaned against a tree.

name: Yew Longbow
description: Strong yet lissome, some say a yew bow won’t fire unless the shot feeds a stomach or ends a monster.

name: Short Bow
description: A short ranged bow for novice archers or those wishing to remain discrete.

name: Twinkling Short Bow
description: A short bow polished to a mirror sheen - for archers swift enough that they don't care about being spotted.

name: Siren's Call
description: When strung, it hums just above hearing. Some archers report strange dreams and uncanny aim.

name: Serrated Cutter
description: A grisly polearm carried often by Havenport guards - Governor Grafston's idea of "justice with teeth."

name: Regal Cleft
description: It's tradition for an apprenticing mainland knight to choose the stone to serve as his halberd's counterweight.

name: Dried Hive
description: A dried out hive of spores, tainting the air with each swing, fungi happy to take root inside a foe's lungs.

name: Caretaker's Ruined Staff
description: A Dasha Caretaker's staff, only grown putrid and diseased. Lulling voices can almost be heard wafting from it.

name: Endless Winter
description: The cold shatters all things – the spirits of men and their bones as well.

name: Stag of Embers
description: A smoldering effigy of occult origins. Whomever holds it speaks for all the forests felled and silenced.

name: Pinwheel Staff
description: A staff made from a wagon wheel and a fetid urn. There are no limits to man's ingenuity.

name: Gnarled Staff
description: The pitiful husk of a tree, once burned by lightning; now it harbors the power to burn grown men to ash.

name: Sinner's Crown
description: For some, atonement is eternal. Shackles cast off, cell opened, but punishment carried with them always.

name: Cursed Crone
description: Some say witches still lurk in Sacra's dark corners, crafting tools out of wood, mold, and unlucky creatures.

name: Falling Sky
description: A stave made from a ring of rock that tumbled down from the heavens, its curve strangely perfect.

name: Spherin
description: The long-dead guardians of the Frozen Peaks carried these staffs - like all of winter held in a clenched fist.

name: False Truth
description: A staff for those who feed on deception, who mimic their meals rather than confront them.

name: Claymore
description: A remnant of simpler times, when one knight faced another in an open field.

name: Icebreaker
description: Used in winter to shear off great blocks of ice, it finds other uses year round.

name: Shattered Sun
description: A design that saw great popularity in Phalen until its designer was caught in bed with the King's nephew.

name: Gnarled Saw
description: If its first swing doesn't sever a limb, a few back and forth motions will do the trick.

name: Summer's Sting
description: The knights of Elgarth test their blade's edge by counting the stalks of wheat they can fell in a single swing.

name: Mayhem
description: The brutal, massive blade of an executioner who has lost their purpose but not their calling.

name: Corpse Smeared Blade
description: Previously wielded by a plagued monstrosity, no amount of scrubbing seems able to remove the stench.

name: Sieger
description: Stamped with the seal of the great smith Tomlin the Third, its blade will cut a falling feather in two.

name: Blister
description: It's said that if a shard of this jagged sword chips off, it will grow in its victim's body, turning their blood to iron.

name: Festering Earth
description: A long buried blade, chewed away by the elements, dragged out of its grave for one final campaign.

name: Freiheit
description: A weapon made famous from its use by freedom fighters during the Monksbane Wars.

name: Divine Scimitar
description: A weapon once radiant with holy purpose now worn down to a fine sinew of steel.

name: Sunbeam
description: The raiders of Darshem only attack at noon, holding these swords aloft to blind enemies with their shine.

name: Scythe of Wretches
description: Before the pestilence rotted their minds and limbs, the Nith used such scythes to harvest swampgrass roots.

name: Hope Shorn
description: The scythe of The Reapers - a sadistic death cult - this unholy weapon whispers a dark thirst.

name: Soul Thresher
description: "Easy to separate a head from a neck, far harder to separate a soul from a body." - Thorn executioner axiom

name: Evening Star
description: No dawn follows the blow of an Evening Star.

name: Fetid Club
description: As one counts the years ringed on a tree trunk, one can count the lives smeared on this massive staff.

name: Paddle
description: A massive paddle.

name: Brutish Cudgel
description: It's an ongoing debate as to whether giants ever roamed the land. Clubs such as these keep the question open.

name: Broken Fang
description: A shattered axe, having long since left pieces of itself buried in skulls and rib cages, but still deadly.

name: Cerim's Skyrend
description: Those who can no longer hear the Sayer over the howl of their own madness should be pitied. Pitied and feared.

name: Midnight Shredder
description: Not all great axes are graceless, brutal weapons. But this one is.

name: Boarskin Reaper
description: There is no piece of nature's bounty and grace that the Boarskin can't defile.

name: Harvester's Dread
description: Before being twisted into Harvesters, many woodsmen would set camp for their families near the forest. Hence the oldest blood on their axes.

name: Spalled Axe
description: The Dasha people of Marin eschew weapons, but their sturdy axes don't care how they're put to use.

name: Gilded Twibill
description: "Paint your axe gold, so that your enemies may mistake the shine in their skull for heaven." - old proverb

name: Short Bardiche
description: It's considered a sign of respect from the Riders of Langold if they face you with the longer of twin axe faces.

name: Huro's Great Axe
description: In the legend of Huro, he used his axe to fell an entire forest, as well as the minor god he angered in doing so.

name: Bandit's Cleaver
description: A heavy iron axe carried often by bandits, and a testament to the fact that not all threats are hollow.

name: Pound of Cadavers
description: The blood, bones, and flesh of fallen warriors woven into a grotesque amalgamation.

name: Tooth of the Ancient One
description: The tooth of an unknown monstrosity. With any luck, its owner is no longer searching for it.

name: Spliced Hammer
description: Originally designed for shattering unruly stones, it seems to take greater pleasure doing the same to skulls.

name: Weeping Earth
description: A tree trunk forms a crude but effective mallet, used by those Risen raiders weighty enough to lift it.

name: Bud of the Everlasting Tree
description: A strange growth cut from a holy tree - it continues to grow with each swing, as if fed by blood.

name: Cinder & Stone
description: Studded with small, strange faces, it's said to be the burial tool for a long lost hill tribe.

name: Festering Cleft
description: A sickly stone affixed to a shaft. Perhaps it will shatter tomorrow. Perhaps never.

name: Simple Torch
description: Held in left hand, brightens dark areas. Time-limited usage, loses durability over time while equipped.

name: Harvester's Bonesaw
description: Pray that the screams of its victims drown out the wet grinding crunch of its progress.

name: Ogre's Club
description: It is like the life of man: nasty, brutish, and short.

name: Unholy Hearth
description: Not all fires bring warmth – some offer instead an everlasting cold.

name: Nail-Laden Club
description: A weapon for those in desperate times. Swing first, swing hard, and hope for the best.

name: Blacksmith’s Folly
description: Its few surviving victims argue over what’s worse – the going in or the pulling back out.

name: Rusty Khopesh
description: The Grifkin goat men model these blades after ram horns - ideal for swift disembowelings.

name: Talwar
description: The weapon of choice for knights of the now-disgraced House of Mar'quat.

name: Scimitar
description: Upon joining the Sailor's Guild, one is issued a compass, an Ashen Sea map, and a scimitar. All will be used.

name: Filleter
description: A fisherman's blade, it severs cleanly and without fuss.

name: Risen Blade
description: A well-weighted scimitar of simple design commonly seen dangling from Risen belts.

name: Etched Yatagan
description: Legend says if you carve the name of your enemy into its hilt, it will only shatter upon their demise.

name: Demon's Key
description: This sword's forked point serves no purpose other than brutality. But, at times, brutality is a purpose.

name: Cerulean Blade
description: The "eye" affixed to its cross guard is more than decorative - it's a counterbalance for precise strikes.

name: Buried Shamshir
description: The ancient method of its forging saw it buried in sand for forty nights. Modern blacksmiths eschew this step.

name: Cleric's Mace
description: A tool of "re-education" often used by the more enthusiastic of Caylen's devotees.

name: Shrike Tree
description: The Balak Taw learn all the worst lessons from their winged brothers - such as how to impale an enemy.

name: Malice
description: Some weapons blur the line between instrument of war and instrument of torture.

name: The Last Word
description: Only once the silence is complete - one crushing blow at a time - will the Cerim's task be fulfilled.

name: Tongue Splitter
description: Designed by Royal Executioner Rochel himself, ideal for deaths delivered without hesitation nor mercy.

name: Royal Hewn Axe
description: The jagged edges of this axe are a particular cruelty, and a reminder of the cost of crossing Phalen.

name: Boarskin Tusk
description: Favored by the Boarskin clan, this rugged axe features a jagged edge mimicking the ferocity of a wild boar's tusks.

name: Hunter's Axe
description: A simple axe carried by hunters to make a fast shelter or a faster kill.

name: Shroudcleaver
description: In your hands it feels light as a feather, sunk into your foe’s skull it feels heavy as the hereafter.

name: Reclaimer
description: Some claim the Harvesters growl “timber” when they cleave a man in two, a vestige of their old selves buried beneath their madness.

name: Ripper
description: A battle axe, well-worn but still sharp. It has cleaved many men and seen what lies within.

name: Jade-Spined Axe
description: "May the grass grow over your bones" - a blessing and a curse uttered by the tribesmen bearing this axe.

name: Butcher's Work
description: One grieves for even the animals laid to rest with such a twisted blade.

name: Hunter's Knife
description: A simple, versatile knife preferred by hunters. If there is a job to be done, it will do it.

name: Midnight Blade
description: The assassins of Myrfall are said to carry such blades, to make short work of those judged unworthy of living.

name: Jackknife
description: "A knife is like a poor secret, whispered deep and often." - old proverb

name: A Singular Purpose
description: A well-crafted dagger is the great leveler - it slides equally into the bellies of drunks and backs of kings.

name: Sacrament's Sin
description: Eight of these spears plunged into the 26th Governor of Sacrament at once, executing her for her crimes.

name: Petaled Spear
description: Its fluted tip is as pleasing to the eye as it is damaging to any tissue it penetrates.

name: Gnarled Harpoon
description: A rustic harpoon designed to bury itself in a whale's hide and not let go.

name: Assegai
description: The men of Arvos use these spears both to keep enemies at bay and test the sinking sands' depth.

name: Coral Piercer
description: A spear of Kylesh design, where emeralds litter the island shoals like iridescent boulders.

name: Needle Spear
description: A common spear, simple but effective, often carried by the Sworn Guards of Sacrament.

name: Proud Lance
description: A wedged jousting lance meant for splintering shields and the bones they protect.

name: Singing Peal
description: Once a year, a troupe of Havenport bards use this hammer for music. The other 364 days its use is violence.

name: Broken Forge
description: It's tradition to use the remnants of an exhausted forge to make one final, monstrous hammer.

name: Brutal Maul
description: It splits stone and skulls alike - a mountain of purpose focused on a single point.

name: Rostock
description: An unsually supple hammer said to originate from the blind Stone Tappers of The Seethe.

name: Grimacing Stone
description: It is unknown if the stone was chiseled into the face of a man, or if a man's skull was transformed into stone.

name: Climber's Pick
description: They can be seen dotting the Frozen Peaks, memorials to those who gave their life to the climb.

name: Amberhusk
description: As the rank mass dribbles away, so too does the wand’s potency, leaving only the stain of sweetness.

name: Fused Coronet
description: All wands are hungry. It’s up to its wielder to determine for what.

name: Nith Gate
description: “One hitch to tie your garter, one weave to warm your bed. One stitch to hold the dark at bay, one knot to crack your head.” - nursery rhyme

name: Entwined Root
description: The more gnarled the tree, the more magic one might find entangled in its roots.

name: Moon Shard
description: It's not your reflection you see in its cracked visage, it's a small sliver of yourself lost forever.

name: Sanglier
description: What magic simmered in the head of a plagued boar? And what sick soul saw fit to craft with it?

name: Flame Becomes Us
description: A strange assemblage of mystical totems, unified only by the dark will of its wielder.

name: Grasping Magus Wand
description: The Born God has many hands – see what damage just one can do.

name: Tarnished Wand
description: In the Phalen religion, magic is blasphemy, so the church’s more mystical adherents simply practice “acts of faith.”

name: Crooked Key
description: Some doors, once opened, can be hard to shut.

name: Molten Clutch
description: The embers should burn its branches. The branches should crush the embers. Magic abhors reason.

name: All Is Known
description: Everything has a price. Even knowledge. Especially knowledge.

name: Rune-Stricken Wand
description: Put your ear to this wand, and you’ll hear dark whispers. Put your eye to it, and you’ll never see again.

name: Hoar Frost Sickle
description: On the Isle of Kull, wheat is ice-shorn with these mystic wands – threshed and frozen for winter in a single swing.

name: Lord of the Muck
description: All wands are unholy, but those made from human remains dally with the most wretched of forces.

name: Cronus
description: A cursed weapon reputed to hold its victims' souls eternally in the moment of their demise.

name: Blood-Rusted Sword
description: Rusted by blood and weary from death, this sword carries out its duty with grim purpose.

name: Governor's Sword
description: The governor's long sword serves as a reminder - to seek peace by any means necessary, including war.

name: Falstead's Fury
description: This towering blade seems heavy with memory - battles won and lost and never fought.

name: Scorched Broadblade
description: A great slab of a sword etched with the stories of an unknown, long dead people.

name: Nith Blade
description: It reeks of the swamp's wretched bargain - a fast death by blade or a slow one by sickness.

name: Governor's Dagger
description: The governor's short sword serves as a promise - to keep his aspirations and his enemies in striking distance.

name: Spirit Edge
description: All of the forest's fury sealed within a blade of pure light. Glowing wisps drift from its surface like willow branches.

name: Wooden Sword
description: A training sword for a squire - used in combat only by the overconfident, the deranged, or those in dire straits.

name: Azure Blade
description: Imperfections in steel traditionally lead to weakness, but this sword's blend of twin metals imparts strength.

name: Whetted Wedge
description: Some swords seek out the tiniest of openings, while others carve their own.

name: Tucked Falcon
description: A falcon relies not on subterfuge not guile, but speed alone. This blade is no different.

name: Siren Queen's Horn
description: A blade forged from the severed horn of a Siren Queen, a creature rarely seen and almost never survived.

name: Moonshaft
description: Shaped in a "cold stone forge," they're said to strike faster beneath the glow of the moon.

name: Reaching Nail
description: The Morgana "Mud Men" Uprising saw much use of these wicked spikes of iron, pin-cushioning Atlan knights.

name: Sinew
description: A rapier of such surgical precision that some claim they can feel a slight tug of it pulling toward a foe's heart.

name: Pig Sticker
description: A blacksmith's wife caught her husband cheating and used one to skewer him toe to crown. The name stuck.

name: Tortured Blade
description: A decayed and pitted sword, blackened by the dark deeds of former masters.

name: Honed Frenzy
description: A sword from the isle of Thorn - you can still smell the saltwater in its hilt.

name: Coiled Dawn
description: A sword that strikes swift as a viper, the air hissing as it's riven.

name: Wind of Death
description: This wickedly long blade is meant to end a fight before a foe has a chance to start one.

name: Unarmed
description: You were born with two fists - might as well put them to use.

name: Emerald Talons
description: Not all craftsmen work the loom or potter’s wheel. The Balak Taw know - violence too is an art.

name: Sun's Scorn
description: Some say the sun is fueled by fire. Others insist it’s rage.

name: Crystal Fist
description: A diamond is harder than bone, and it’s happy to prove it.

name: Deadfang
description: Late at night, when the world is quiet and still, you can faintly hear it pleading for its next meal.

name: Ironstud Interrogators
description: The Knights of Tanth popularized this design for both open combat and backroom “questioning”. Answers tend to dislodge as fast as teeth.

name: Shellguard Gauntlets
description: One step above tying rocks to your fists - these gloves are meant to pulp and pulverize. Grace is for dancers.

name: Gossamer Gauntlets
description: With steel spun fine as a spider’s thread, these blades rip wounds as gaping as a bear's bite.

name: Cursed Unguis
description: Long ago a forgotten warrior made a dark bargain, trading flesh for power. This is all that remains.

name: Reibach's Claws
description: It cuts as clean as a surgeon’s scalpel, only its patients rarely recover.

name: Nith Grovebinders
description: Wood rots, cloth frays, stone crumbles, but fury smolders eternal.

name: Claw of the Crescent Moon
description: When a body is discovered at daybreak with three deep rents down its back, authorities know to leave well enough alone.

name: The Dawn Never Comes
description: Cerim texts contain no reference to a life after the Pestilence is gone.

name: Woven Wraps
description: Simple cloth wraps for those who trust that their fists are plenty hard enough.

name: Bleeder's Delight
description: Daggers designed for rapid, deep punctures to desanguinate with ease.

name: Chipped Daggers
description: It's a cruel irony that the more a dagger decays, the more viciously it tears into flesh.

name: Brothers Keepers
description: "Which blade will stand watch while the other sleeps? Neither, my blades never rest." - old Kylesh proverb

name: Rogue Messer
description: A rogue's dagger should be forged as a rogue's life is lived - not too short, not too long, and ending swiftly.

name: Ash Soothed Daggers
description: Ashes from the forge are rubbed into their surface, dulling their shine but sharpening their point.
